---
title: "Bayesian Prior Justification Report"
subtitle: "`r params$trial_name`"
author: "`r params$author`"
date: "`r params$date`"

params:
  prior:         !r NULL
  conflict:      !r NULL
  sensitivity:   !r NULL
  trial_name:    "Clinical Trial"
  sponsor:       "Sponsor"
  author:        "Biostatistics"
  date:          ""
  notes:         ""
  prior_plot:    !r NULL
  overlay_plot:  !r NULL
  tornado_plot:  !r NULL
  heatmap_plot:  !r NULL

output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    code_folding: hide
    self_contained: true
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    number_sections: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
  word_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    fig_width: 6.5
    fig_height: 4
    fig_caption: true

---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 6.5, fig.height = 4)

library(bayprior)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)

pr  <- params$prior
cd  <- params$conflict
sa  <- params$sensitivity
has_prior <- !is.null(pr)
has_conf  <- !is.null(cd)
has_sens  <- !is.null(sa)

# Detect output format from knitr — avoids needing a custom param that
# rmarkdown::render() rejects when passed alongside a format object.
is_docx <- isTRUE(knitr::opts_knit$get("rmarkdown.pandoc.to") == "docx")

# Pre-captured plot objects from the Shiny session.
# When not NULL and are ggplot objects, these reflect exactly what the analyst
# saw during the session. Plotly widgets (from renderPlotly) cannot be printed
# to static formats, so we guard with inherits(..., "gg").
pp_prior_plot   <- params$prior_plot
pp_overlay_plot <- params$overlay_plot
pp_tornado_plot <- params$tornado_plot
pp_heatmap_plot <- params$heatmap_plot

# ── Safe helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Self-contained: no dependency on %||% or rlang, which are absent from
# rmarkdown::render()'s fresh env when called from inside a Shiny app.

.null_or <- function(x, default) {
  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0L) default else x
}

.safe_round <- function(x, digits = 4) {
  x <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
  round(x, digits)
}

.safe_chr <- function(x, fallback = "\u2014") {
  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0L || (length(x) == 1L && is.na(x)))
    return(fallback)
  as.character(x[[1L]])
}

.fs <- function(s, nm) {
  v <- s[[nm]]
  if (is.null(v) || length(v) == 0L) NA_real_
  else suppressWarnings(as.numeric(v[[1L]]))
}

.params_df <- function(prior_obj) {
  p <- prior_obj$params
  if (is.null(p) || length(p) == 0L)
    return(data.frame(Parameter = character(0L), Value = character(0L),
                      stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
  if (identical(prior_obj$dist, "mixture")) {
    w <- prior_obj$weights
    if (is.null(w)) w <- p$weights
    if (is.null(w)) w <- numeric(0L)
    return(data.frame(
      Parameter        = paste0("weight[", seq_along(w), "]"),
      Value            = as.character(round(w, 6L)),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  vals <- tryCatch(unlist(p), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(vals) || !is.numeric(vals))
    return(data.frame(Parameter = character(0L), Value = character(0L),
                      stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
  data.frame(
    Parameter        = names(vals),
    Value            = as.character(.safe_round(vals, 6L)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

# Suppresses the expected mixture-family density warning inside plot calls.
.safe_plot <- function(expr_fn, msg = "Plot could not be rendered") {
  withCallingHandlers(
    tryCatch(expr_fn(), error = function(e) cat(msg, ":", conditionMessage(e), "\n")),
    warning = function(w) {
      if (grepl("different distribution families", conditionMessage(w), fixed = TRUE))
        invokeRestart("muffleWarning")
    }
  )
}

# Format-aware callout — blockquote works in HTML, PDF, and Word.
.callout <- function(text, type = "note") {
  label <- switch(type,
    note      = "Note",
    warning   = "Warning",
    important = "Important",
    tip       = "Tip",
    "Note"
  )
  cat(paste0("> **", label, ":** ", text, "\n\n"))
}
```

---

## Executive Summary

This report documents the Bayesian prior specification for study
**`r params$trial_name`**, prepared by **`r params$author`**
(`r params$sponsor`) and dated `r format(Sys.Date(), "%d %B %Y")`.

```{r exec-table}
if (has_prior) {
  s <- pr$fit_summary
  df_exec <- data.frame(
    Item = c("Distribution family", "Elicitation method",
             "Expert / source", "Prior mean", "Prior SD",
             "95% Credible interval"),
    Value = c(
      toupper(.safe_chr(pr$dist)),
      .safe_chr(pr$method),
      .safe_chr(pr$expert_id),
      as.character(.safe_round(.fs(s, "mean"), 4)),
      as.character(.safe_round(.fs(s, "sd"),   4)),
      paste0("[", .safe_round(.fs(s, "q025"), 4),
             ",  ", .safe_round(.fs(s, "q975"), 4), "]")
    ),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  rownames(df_exec) <- NULL
  kable(df_exec, col.names = c("Item", "Value"), align = "ll",
        caption = "Prior specification summary", row.names = FALSE)
}
```

```{r notes-block, results='asis'}
note_text <- .null_or(params$notes, "")
if (nzchar(note_text)) {
  .callout(paste0("Statistician\u2019s notes: ", note_text), "note")
}
```

---

## Trial Information

```{r trial-info}
df_trial <- data.frame(
  Field = c("Trial / Protocol", "Sponsor", "Statistician", "Report date"),
  Value = c(
    .safe_chr(params$trial_name),
    .safe_chr(params$sponsor),
    .safe_chr(params$author),
    format(Sys.Date(), "%d %B %Y")
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
rownames(df_trial) <- NULL
kable(df_trial, col.names = c("Field", "Value"), align = "ll",
      row.names = FALSE)
```

---

## Prior Specification

### Elicitation Methodology

```{r elicitation-method}
if (has_prior) {
  df_elic <- data.frame(
    Attribute = c("Quantity", "Distribution", "Method", "Expert / Source"),
    Value     = c(
      .safe_chr(pr$label),
      toupper(.safe_chr(pr$dist)),
      .safe_chr(pr$method),
      .safe_chr(pr$expert_id)
    ),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  rownames(df_elic) <- NULL
  kable(df_elic, col.names = c("Attribute", "Value"), align = "ll",
        row.names = FALSE)
}
```

### Fitted Parameters

```{r fitted-params}
if (has_prior) {
  df <- .params_df(pr)
  rownames(df) <- NULL
  if (nrow(df) > 0L) {
    kable(df, col.names = c("Parameter", "Value"),
          caption = "Fitted hyperparameters", align = "lr",
          row.names = FALSE)
  } else {
    cat("Hyperparameters not available for this prior type.\n")
  }
}
```

### Prior Summary Statistics

```{r prior-summary}
if (has_prior) {
  s <- pr$fit_summary
  df_sum <- data.frame(
    Statistic = c("Mean", "Standard deviation",
                  "2.5th percentile", "Median", "97.5th percentile"),
    Value     = as.character(.safe_round(
      c(.fs(s, "mean"), .fs(s, "sd"),
        .fs(s, "q025"), .fs(s, "q500"), .fs(s, "q975")), 5)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  rownames(df_sum) <- NULL
  kable(df_sum, caption = "Prior distribution summary statistics",
        align = "lr", row.names = FALSE)
}
```

### Prior Density

```{r prior-density, fig.cap="Elicited prior distribution. Shaded region = 95% credible interval; dashed line = prior mean."}
if (has_prior) {
  if (!is.null(pp_prior_plot) && inherits(pp_prior_plot, "gg")) {
    print(pp_prior_plot)
  } else {
    .safe_plot(function() plot(pr), "Prior density plot could not be rendered")
  }
}
```

---

## Prior-Data Conflict Assessment

```{r conflict-check, results='asis'}
if (!has_conf) {
  .callout("No prior-data conflict diagnostics were computed for this report.", "warning")
}
```

```{r conflict-table}
if (has_conf) {
  df_conf <- data.frame(
    Diagnostic = c(
      "Box's prior predictive p-value",
      "Surprise index (standardised distance)",
      "KL divergence (prior || likelihood)",
      "Bhattacharyya overlap coefficient",
      "Conflict severity"
    ),
    Value = c(
      as.character(.safe_round(cd$box_pvalue,          4)),
      as.character(.safe_round(cd$surprise_index,      4)),
      as.character(.safe_round(cd$kl_prior_likelihood, 4)),
      as.character(.safe_round(cd$overlap,             4)),
      toupper(.safe_chr(cd$conflict_severity))
    ),
    Interpretation = c(
      ifelse(isTRUE(cd$conflict_flag),
             paste0("Conflict detected (p < alpha = ", cd$alpha, ")"),
             "No conflict detected"),
      {
        si <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(cd$surprise_index))
        if (is.na(si)) "Could not compute"
        else if (si > 3) "High surprise (> 3 SD)"
        else if (si > 2) "Moderate surprise"
        else "Within expected range"
      },
      {
        kl <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(cd$kl_prior_likelihood))
        if (is.na(kl)) "Could not compute"
        else if (kl > 1) "Large divergence"
        else "Acceptable divergence"
      },
      {
        ov <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(cd$overlap))
        if (is.na(ov)) "Could not compute"
        else if (ov < 0.3) "Low overlap \u2014 concern"
        else if (ov < 0.6) "Moderate overlap"
        else "Good overlap"
      },
      toupper(.safe_chr(cd$conflict_severity))
    ),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  rownames(df_conf) <- NULL
  kable(df_conf, caption = "Prior-data conflict diagnostics",
        align = "llr", row.names = FALSE)
}
```

```{r conflict-recommendation, results='asis'}
if (has_conf) {
  sev  <- .safe_chr(cd$conflict_severity, fallback = "none")
  type <- switch(sev, none = "tip", mild = "warning", severe = "important", "note")
  .callout(.safe_chr(cd$recommendation), type)
}
```

### Prior - Likelihood - Posterior Overlay

```{r overlay-plot, fig.cap="Overlay of prior density, scaled likelihood, and resulting posterior."}
if (has_conf && has_prior) {
  if (!is.null(pp_overlay_plot) && inherits(pp_overlay_plot, "gg")) {
    print(pp_overlay_plot)
  } else {
    .safe_plot(
      function() plot_prior_likelihood(pr, cd$data_summary, show_posterior = TRUE),
      "Overlay plot could not be rendered"
    )
  }
}
```

---

## Sensitivity Analysis

```{r sensitivity-check, results='asis'}
if (!has_sens) {
  .callout("No sensitivity analysis was conducted for this report.", "warning")
}
```

### Influence of Prior Hyperparameters

```{r sensitivity-influence}
if (has_sens) {
  sc  <- sa$influence_scores
  nms <- names(sc)
  if (is.null(nms) || length(nms) == 0L)
    nms <- paste0("target_", seq_along(sc))
  # rownames() must be explicitly cleared — data.frame() does not accept
  # row.names=NULL as an argument, and named vectors become row names silently.
  df_sens <- data.frame(
    Target    = nms,
    Range     = as.character(.safe_round(sc, 5)),
    Sensitive = ifelse(is.na(sc) | sc > 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  rownames(df_sens) <- NULL
  kable(df_sens,
        caption = "Posterior sensitivity to prior hyperparameters",
        align = "lrr", row.names = FALSE)
}
```

### Tornado Plot

```{r tornado, fig.cap="Range of each posterior quantity across the hyperparameter grid. Wider bars = greater prior influence."}
if (has_sens) {
  if (!is.null(pp_tornado_plot) && inherits(pp_tornado_plot, "gg")) {
    print(pp_tornado_plot)
  } else {
    .safe_plot(function() plot_tornado(sa), "Tornado plot could not be rendered")
  }
}
```

### Hyperparameter Influence Heatmap

```{r heatmap, fig.cap="Posterior mean across the two-dimensional hyperparameter grid."}
if (has_sens) {
  # pp_heatmap_plot may be a Plotly widget (from Shiny renderPlotly) which
  # cannot be printed to static formats. Guard with inherits(..., "gg").
  if (!is.null(pp_heatmap_plot) && inherits(pp_heatmap_plot, "gg")) {
    print(pp_heatmap_plot)
  } else {
    .safe_plot(
      function() plot_sensitivity(sa, target = sa$target[[1L]]),
      "Heatmap could not be rendered"
    )
  }
}
```

---

## Regulatory Compliance Checklist

```{r compliance}
df_comp <- data.frame(
  Requirement = c(
    "Prior elicitation method documented",
    "Distribution family and parameters specified",
    "Expert / source of prior identified",
    "Prior density plot provided",
    "Prior-data conflict assessed",
    "Conflict diagnostic statistics reported",
    "Sensitivity analysis performed",
    "Sensitivity visualisations provided",
    "Alternative priors considered",
    "Regulatory report generated"
  ),
  Status = c(
    ifelse(has_prior, "Complete",  "Missing"),
    ifelse(has_prior, "Complete",  "Missing"),
    ifelse(has_prior, "Complete",  "Missing"),
    ifelse(has_prior, "Complete",  "Missing"),
    ifelse(has_conf,  "Complete",  "Not run"),
    ifelse(has_conf,  "Complete",  "Not run"),
    ifelse(has_sens,  "Complete",  "Not run"),
    ifelse(has_sens,  "Complete",  "Not run"),
    ifelse(has_sens,  "See above", "Not run"),
    "Complete"
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
rownames(df_comp) <- NULL
kable(df_comp, align = "ll", caption = "FDA/EMA regulatory compliance checklist",
      row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r compliance-note, results='asis'}
.callout(paste0(
  "This checklist maps to requirements in the FDA Draft Guidance on Bayesian ",
  "Statistical Methods (2026) and the EMA Reflection Paper on the Use of ",
  "Bayesian Statistics in the Evaluation of Medicinal Products."
), "note")
```

---

## Appendix: Session Information

```{r session-info}
si <- sessionInfo()
df_sess <- data.frame(
  Item  = c("R version", "bayprior version", "rmarkdown version",
            "Platform", "Date"),
  Value = c(
    paste(si$R.version$major, si$R.version$minor, sep = "."),
    tryCatch(as.character(utils::packageVersion("bayprior")),
             error = function(e) "unknown"),
    tryCatch(as.character(utils::packageVersion("rmarkdown")),
             error = function(e) "unknown"),
    si$platform,
    format(Sys.Date(), "%d %B %Y")
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
rownames(df_sess) <- NULL
kable(df_sess, align = "ll", row.names = FALSE)
```

---

*Report generated by the **bayprior** R package using `prior_report()`.
Methods follow O'Hagan et al. (2006), Box (1980), Schmidli et al. (2014),
Ibrahim & Chen (2000), and the FDA Draft Guidance on Bayesian Methods in
Clinical Trials (2026).*
